title: Oracle Live Slowness Triage
description: Identify sessions, waits, and SQL causing current performance degradation
author: A. Simmons

session_summary.description: Active session and wait class snapshot

interactive_hints.hot_sql_by_elapsed: Run when active sessions show User I/O or CPU waits — identifies the specific SQL driving them
interactive_hints.active_locks: Run when active sessions show enqueue or lock waits — maps the blocking chain
interactive_hints.sql_execution_plan: Run after identifying a specific sql_id to confirm index usage or full table scan
interactive_hints.redo_log_activity: Run when log file sync waits are prominent — investigates redo generation and commit patterns
interactive_hints.stop: Stop here and generate the final analysis with results gathered so far

hot_sql_by_elapsed.description: Top SQL by elapsed time and buffer gets

interactive_hints.sql_execution_plan: Run to get the execution plan for the top sql_id — confirms whether a full table scan or bad index choice is the cause
interactive_hints.active_locks: Run if lock contention also appeared in the session summary
interactive_hints.redo_log_activity: Run if redo/log waits appeared alongside the SQL
interactive_hints.stop: Stop here and generate the final analysis with results gathered so far

active_locks.description: Blocking sessions and lock wait chains

interactive_hints.hot_sql_by_elapsed: Run alongside lock analysis to see if a specific SQL is both slow and holding locks
interactive_hints.stop: Stop here and generate the final analysis with results gathered so far

sql_execution_plan.description: Execution plan for a specific sql_id

interactive_hints.stop: Stop here and generate the final analysis with results gathered so far

redo_log_activity.description: Redo generation rate and log file sync waits

interactive_hints.stop: Stop here and generate the final analysis with results gathered so far
